posts.author: Quicko
posts.text: Tax filing in quicko — Hello @Abhijeet_Rajgor I understand you are trying to prefill from ITD. The Income tax department have yet not enabled this feature for AY 2026-27. This does not work currently on Income tax portal as well. I request you to wait for sometime till the ITD enables this feature. For now, you can add the data manually and prepare your ITR. Hope, this helps!

posts.author: Quicko
posts.text: Tax filing in quicko — Hello @Somil_Gupta01 You can import all trades from Zerodha on Quicko in a single click, prepare your ITR and e-file. You can visit it.quicko.com to prepare your ITR. Thanks

posts.author: Quicko
posts.text: Tax implication for physically settled stock ITM option — @Jay_Mundra Yes, since the net profit is zero so there would not be any tax on it. Thanks

posts.author: Jay_Mundra
posts.text: Tax implication for physically settled stock ITM option — Hey, suppose I have traded these : BOSCH 25000 CE short: at premium 15000 BOSCH 36500 CE long: at premium: 3500 settlement price is suppose 40000, so that both the options are OTM. net profit = 15000 - 3500 -(40000-25000) + (40000-36500) = 15000-3500-15000+3500 = 0 What would be tax implication of this trade? It should be 0, since my net profit is 0, right?
